required_complexity_patterns.notes: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value
Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words
Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input
Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers
At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element

Full name

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Required: At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element Reference DOM (Duplicate name attributes): Two distinct fields share the same name attribute (invalid but seen in the wild) Reference DOM (Asterisk in sibling span): Required marker asterisk lives in sibling span, not inside label text Reference DOM (SVG icon labels): Label contains SVG icon; accessible name via aria-labelledby only Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: readonly-prefill, glued-label-text, div-role-textbox, split-phone, aria-label-only. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: two-column grid without card wrapper. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Duplicate name attributes; Asterisk in sibling span; SVG icon labels.
